agent_axis_findings: F14 (design stall): adapter:codex-local timed out (TimeoutExpired) at the design step. The codex CLI process was invoked with --sandbox read-only --ephemeral --output-last-message. No agent output was returned (agent_fact_created=false). Primary agent-axis evidence: local_cli_timeout is an adapter-layer failure, not a return-shape failure.

IAP closure (forbidden 'status' key): agent-object:coo returned parent_goal_delta_status as a top-level key in its closure return. COO is the declared performer for the closure step. The COO used this field name despite the adapter's forbidden-key enforcement. This is an Agent-axis return-shape violation: the returned payload contained a forbidden key.

IAP QA/inspector probes incomplete: agent-object:qa (code-attack-qa) and agent-object:inspector (evidence-integrity) both reported that the focused checker and mutation RED could not be executed because no writable temporary directory was available in their sandbox environments. This is an Agent-axis execution constraint, not a Brick contract failure.

dashboard-productization-0612/b: agent-object:dev invoked npm/node (or equivalent) inside the worktree, generating support/dashboard/node_modules/ files. The retry produced the identical error, indicating the agent did not adapt strategy between runs.

adapter-30-s1-park: agent-object:dev attempted to write support/checkers/check_building_lifecycle_path_shape.py outside its declared write_scope. The error prefix write_observation_out_of_scope confirms the adapter's scope guard fired, not a filesystem permission error.

f10 (forbidden 'movement' key): agent-object:dev placed 'movement' key inside handoff_refs in its work return. 'movement' is a Link-vocabulary key forbidden in Agent returns per closed AgentFact discipline. This is an Agent-axis boundary violation: the Agent returned Link-domain vocabulary.

provider-ladder-fleet-presets-0612: agent-object:dev ran a process that wrote to project/brick-protocol/status/inbox/ (checker fixture vessel path). This is outside declared write_scope. The fact that the path contains a timestamp-named fixture file confirms the agent ran a full checker invocation that created projection fixtures.

Read-only inspection of raw/, work/task.md, work/step-outputs/, capture/events.jsonl, evidence/claim_trace/ per declared template method. No files edited.

work/declared-building-plan.json for any building: write_scope was not read from the plan files.
brick_axis_findings: F14 (design stall): task.md scope is broad — 4 source modules to read + 3 deliverables (fix writers + reconciliation verb + checker pin) + proof in single design step. The operator notes that a bounded statement walked in 2min vs 60min stall; suggests the original task weight was a contributing factor alongside provider performance.

IAP closure (forbidden 'status' key): The closure Brick step (brick-iap-dashboard-sink-passport-0612-closure) accepted COO returns with parent_goal_delta_status at the top level. The adapter's forbidden-key validator rejected the key because it contains 'status'. The closing Brick or building plan did not explicitly prohibit parent_goal_delta_status by name, creating ambiguity for COO. The template pattern (MEMORY.md: 'COO 직접' closure pattern) appears to have included this field.

adapter-30-s1-park (write-scope mismatch): task.md deliverable 5 explicitly requires editing support/checkers/check_building_lifecycle_path_shape.py, but that path was not included in the building's declared write_scope; the task contract (Brick) and the write_scope (Brick enforcement layer) were not aligned.

dashboard-productization-0612/b (node_modules): The task required npm/node execution on the dashboard, which generates node_modules. The write_scope (support/*) may have covered node_modules by glob, but the adapter's forbidden-path enforcement blocked it. The task did not specify a temp-copy or separate npm step strategy; the Brick did not account for the node_modules write path.

f10 (forbidden 'movement' key): If the work Brick task statement or pattern instructed the dev to return handoff guidance including a 'movement' key in handoff_refs, the Brick template may have induced the violation. This cannot be confirmed without inspecting the work Brick template for f10.

provider-ladder-fleet-presets-0612 (checker fixture write): the task required running full-gate checker which internally creates checker-projection-fixture-vessel in project/brick-protocol/status/inbox/; this path was outside the declared write_scope. The Brick did not declare the fixture path or instruct temp-copy execution.
link_axis_findings: All hold states were recorded as adapter-error frontiers, not as incorrect Link gate or Movement decisions. No false-hold (gate erroneously blocking a valid return) or false-pass (gate releasing an invalid return) was observed in the ledger evidence.
IAP building: Link movements in capture/events.jsonl are all 'forward' with reason_ref=caller-supplied-link-facts; Link did not choose Movement; no erroneous Link gate action was observed.

adapter-30-s1-park: the building name implies a park disposition but the actual recorded frontier is adapter-error, not a parked frontier. If the building was relabeled as 'park' externally (by the operator), that mislabeling is outside the Link evidence layer — no park record shape was observed in the building's own raw evidence.

f10 (movement key): the forbidden 'movement' key appeared in handoff_refs of the agent's return, NOT in Link records themselves. The Link did not execute; the adapter caught the violation at the return-validation layer before any Link event was recorded.
No reroute, budget exhaustion, false gate, or Link event mismatch was identified across the scoped buildings.

frequency_patterns: Write-scope violations (3 buildings: provider-ladder-fleet-presets, adapter-30-s1-park, dashboard-productization-0612/b): Brick write_scope declarations systematically do not account for all files that the task's deliverables require agents to touch. Pattern suggests write_scope should be validated against task deliverables before building dispatch.

Forbidden-key return violations (2 confirmed incidents: iap closure 'status', f10 'movement'): Agents returning vocabulary from excluded domains (Link keys, status fields). Both are Agent-axis violations; in iap the Brick template may have induced it. Pattern suggests the forbidden-key set needs to be explicitly listed in task instructions, not assumed to be known from protocol alone.

Large-session Agent timeout (1 confirmed in raw + operator-noted ephemeral stalls): F14 design timed out at 2026-06-12T13:03:43Z; operator notes fleet-preset stalls (unrecorded, ephemeral) on the same night. Small probes stayed fast. Pattern consistent with late-night provider degradation at large context, but Brick task weight is a contributing factor.

support_leak_findings: IAP building: three inspector/QA runs confirmed that checker fixture-vessel creation (project/checker-projection-fixture-vessel) was blocked by the read-only sandbox; this is a support surface constraint surfacing as a gap in proof coverage, not a support content leak.

f10 (movement key): 'movement' appearing in handoff_refs of an Agent return is a Link-vocabulary leak into the Agent axis — the Agent asserted Link semantics. This is a support-seam discipline violation recorded in the adapter-error evidence.
